strengths: 20 years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Recent AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows
concerns: No director/principal-level scope: career history is senior/staff IC roles with no evidence of org-wide technical strategy or influencing executive stakeholders
No recognized AI expertise — no AI-related advanced degree, publications, or shipped AI products beyond recent tooling contributions
Cloud experience is application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deploys) rather than cloud platform strategy or advising strategic customers

key_factors.factor: AI strategy and recognized AI expertise
15+ years engineering experience with entrepreneurial background

response.justification: The role's core competencies are AI strategy leadership, cloud platform vision, director-level influence, and recognized AI expertise. The candidate meets the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 20 years of engineering experience, with genuine entrepreneurial evidence (founded and still runs GISCollective). However, the preferred qualifications are largely absent: there is no advanced AI-related degree, no demonstrated AI products or platforms of note (the MCP/agentic tooling at re:cinq since April 2025 is early and unproven at strategic scale), and no evidence of advising strategic customers, setting product-line technical direction, or operating at director level — the strongest titles are Senior/Staff engineer. Cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is implementation-focused rather than strategic. Solid senior/staff engineer, but the evidence does not support a Principal Engineer AI-strategy hire.
